model.label: Claude Sonnet 4.6
model.description: Anthropic • Fast flagship Claude model for coding, analysis, and high-throughput agent use.

pick.rationale: Michigan holds a commanding ~13-point adjusted efficiency margin advantage over Texas Tech (KenPom #2, AdjEM +38.38 vs. KenPom #19, AdjEM +25.19), anchored by the nation's 2nd-best defense (AdjDE 88.4) that is perfectly positioned to neutralize a Red Raiders offense now operating without All-American forward JT Toppin, who tore his ACL in February and is out for the season. Texas Tech's Christian Anderson is healthy and available after his groin scare, but the loss of Toppin — their leading scorer at 21.8 PPG — leaves a significant hole that forces Anderson to carry an unsustainable offensive burden against the toughest defensive assignment he has faced all year. Michigan's Aday Mara (Big Ten Defensive Player of the Year) creates a severe interior matchup problem that Texas Tech's depleted frontcourt cannot answer, and the Wolverines' 31-2 record, Big Ten battle-testing, and elite two-way efficiency profile reflect a program operating at a level that Texas Tech's 22-10 squad — built without its best player for the final stretch — simply cannot match.

reasoningStep.title: Michigan vs. Texas Tech Sweet 16 Matchup Analysis
reasoningStep.summary: Michigan is a clear and confident pick based on a commanding ~13-point efficiency gap, the nation's 2nd-best defense, and Texas Tech's critical personnel loss in JT Toppin. The matchup strongly favors Michigan's elite two-way profile against a Red Raiders offense that has been significantly weakened heading into the Sweet 16.
evidence: KenPom: Michigan #2 (AdjEM +38.38, AdjO 126.7, AdjD 88.4) vs. Texas Tech #19 (AdjEM +25.19, AdjO 125.1, AdjD 99.9) — a ~13-point efficiency gap favoring Michigan.
Torvik: Michigan #2 (AdjOE 127.7, AdjDE 90.5) vs. Texas Tech #12 (AdjOE 126.3, AdjDE 98.8) — Michigan's defense is 8+ points better per 100 possessions.
JT Toppin (All-American, 21.8 PPG) tore his ACL in February and is out for the season, severely depleting Texas Tech's offensive ceiling and frontcourt depth.
Christian Anderson cleared from groin injury and available, but carries a dramatically heavier offensive load without Toppin — running into the 2nd-best defense in the country.

Michigan's Aday Mara (Big Ten Defensive Player of the Year) creates a severe interior matchup problem; Yaxel Lendeborg's ankle sprain confirmed non-disqualifying with Michigan at near full strength and +360 national championship odds (2nd-best in field).

toolCalls.summary: Returned combined Torvik/KenPom ratings for 2 teams.
